body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: As a parent, I find the School of Engineering's communication regarding my child's academic progress to be superb and genuinely reassuring. Semester progress reports are issued promptly through the parent portal, and attendance alerts are sent in real time, allowing me to stay informed and intervene early when needed. I particularly value the parent information sessions organized each semester, where faculty and administrators present academic expectations, available support services, and the student performance outlook in detail. The level of transparency and proactive communication from the Academic Affairs Office has greatly increased my confidence in the institution's commitment to student success and parental partnership.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: Teaching quality in my Mechanical Engineering program at the School of Engineering has been reasonable overall, though noticeably inconsistent across different modules this semester. Several lecturers deliver material with clarity and genuine enthusiasm, making those sessions highly productive and intellectually stimulating for the class. However, a few courses suffer from disorganized delivery, limited interaction, and minimal feedback on submitted work, which hampers the learning experience considerably. I would highlight the school to standardize teaching quality benchmarks and conduct regular peer evaluations to ensure a more consistent and high-quality educational experience for all students across every module and year level.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: The career preparation support provided by the Career Center at the School of Engineering has been superb and has made a tangible difference to my professional readiness and employment prospects as I approach graduation. Resume writing workshops, mock interviews, and industry networking events have been well-organized, highly practical, and attended by credible industry professionals who provided genuinely actionable feedback. The internship matching program is effective, with clear communication about available placements and strong follow-up support for students during their internship periods. I commend the Career Center's proactive approach and its strong relationships with employers, which have opened real opportunities for students across all programs.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: My confidence in the School of Engineering's ability to ensure problematic levels of campus safety and student welfare has been significantly diminished by several incidents and structural shortcomings reported to me by my child. Security coverage appears insufficient in certain areas of campus, particularly during late evening hours, and lighting in several pathways is inadequate and has been reported without timely resolution. Mental health support services are critically under-resourced — my child was advised to wait over three weeks for a counseling appointment during a period of acute personal difficulty, which is simply unacceptable. I urge the Student Affairs Office to treat student welfare as a non-negotiable priority and invest accordingly, without further delay.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: The financial transparency and management provided by the Finance Department at the School of Engineering has been remarkable, and I genuinely applaud the clarity with which tuition fees, scholarship entitlements, and payment deadlines are communicated to both students and their families. Fee breakdowns are detailed, accurate, and issued well in advance of payment deadlines, allowing adequate financial planning time. The installment payment option is a genuinely thoughtful and practical provision that reduces financial stress without compromising the institution's financial management requirements. Scholarship disbursements have been timely and confirmed through official notifications, reinforcing trust in the university's commitment to honoring its financial obligations to students and families.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Engineering functions at a reasonable level that provides access to essential information but lacks the polish and reliability expected of a modern institutional digital platform. Grade and attendance data are generally accessible, though refresh rates can be slow and the interface requires familiarity before becoming navigable. The portal is not optimized for mobile devices, which is a significant limitation given that most parents primarily use smartphones for digital access. Push notification functionality exists but is inconsistently triggered, meaning important updates are sometimes missed. A targeted UX improvement initiative — focusing on mobile responsiveness, navigation clarity, and reliable notifications — would greatly increase parental engagement and satisfaction with the digital experience.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling at the School of Engineering is reasonable, covering essential advising needs without providing the depth and personalization that would truly distinguish the service. My child has found their assigned advisor to be approachable and knowledgeable on the whole, though appointment availability is sometimes limited during peak periods of the academic calendar. The counseling provided tends to be reactive — responding to student-initiated queries rather than proactively identifying and addressing potential challenges before they escalate. A more structured check-in model, combined with a larger and better-trained advising team, would significantly improve the consistency and impact of academic counseling across the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: The student support services offered by the Student Affairs Office at the School of Engineering have been superb and have made a genuine difference to my overall well-being and university experience throughout the semester. Counseling services are readily accessible, professionally delivered, and clearly communicated to students through multiple channels including the student portal, orientation sessions, and notice boards. The range of support available — from academic counseling to mental health resources and financial hardship assistance — reflects a comprehensive understanding of the diverse challenges students face during their academic journey. I also recognize the regular student welfare check-ins organized by the office, which foster a sense of community and belonging on campus.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: The physical facilities at the School of Engineering, under the responsibility of the Facilities Management, present inadequate conditions that negatively impact the daily learning experience of students. Several lecture halls have malfunctioning air conditioning systems that make studying in warm months genuinely uncomfortable and distracting. Toilet facilities in older buildings are poorly maintained and frequently reported without timely resolution from the facilities team. Study spaces are chronically insufficient relative to the student population, particularly during examination periods when demand peaks significantly. I urge the university to prioritize facilities maintenance budgets, establish a transparent issue-reporting system, and expand study space capacity as part of its next capital improvement plan.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: Reflecting on my child's full year of study at the School of Engineering, I find myself with insufficient concerns about the institution's ability to consistently deliver on the educational and welfare commitments it promotes to prospective students and their families. Persistent issues with teaching quality in certain departments, administrative responsiveness, digital platform reliability, and student support capacity have collectively made a difficult academic transition more challenging than it needed to be. I would strongly urge university leadership to prioritize meaningful engagement with the concerns raised in parent feedback, establish clear accountability mechanisms, and communicate specific improvement actions transparently to the parent community in the coming semester.
